text.title: Canonical ServiceResult Class
text.summary: One ServiceResult class for the entire codebase; all service objects return it.

rule.statement: Define exactly one ServiceResult at app/services/service_result.rb. All service objects return it from #call. CI must fail if a second definition is found. Use success?/failure? as the only API — never ok?, result?, or error?.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent slightly different ServiceResult shapes per service file (sometimes result.ok?, sometimes result.success?). A divergent API means every controller must handle multiple shapes. Lock the API in CI with a simple grep.

example.code:
module Api module V1 class UsersController < ApplicationController def create result = Users::CreateService.new( params: user_params, current_user: current_user ).call
if result.success? render json: UserSerializer.new(result.data), status: :created else render json: { errors: result.errors }, status: :unprocessable_entity end end
private
def user_params params.require(:user).permit(:name, :email, :password) end end end end
